header.title: Verdiyan Legal
header.description: A law firm providing legal services to both individuals and businesses. Our practice is built on professional expertise, strategic legal thinking, and clear communication with our clients.

specializations.title: Expertise
specializations.description: We provide legal services across a broad range of practice areas. Our law firm assists clients with legal matters related to property rights, contractual relationships, inheritance, and other areas of civil law. We prepare and review contracts, represent clients in legal disputes, and protect their personal rights and property interests.

civil.title: Civil Law
civil.description1: We provide legal advice and representation in civil law matters, particularly in contractual relationships, protection of property rights, damage claims, inheritance matters, and other civil law issues.
civil.description2: Our services include contract drafting and review, legal analysis, representation in court proceedings, and out-of-court dispute resolution.

commercial.title: Commercial Law
commercial.description1: In the area of commercial law, we provide legal services to entrepreneurs and companies. Our practice focuses on commercial contracts, corporate matters, company formation, changes in corporate structure, and legal support in business transactions.
commercial.description2: We represent clients in negotiations with business partners, in court proceedings, and in the resolution of commercial disputes.

family.title: Family Law
family.description1: Family law requires not only legal expertise but also a sensitive and thoughtful approach. We provide legal assistance in matters such as divorce, child custody arrangements, child support, visitation rights, and division of marital property.
family.description2: Our goal is to help clients find solutions that are legally sound while respecting the specific circumstances of each family situation.

intellectual.title: Intellectual Property Law
intellectual.description1: We provide legal protection in the field of intellectual property, including copyright, trademarks, industrial designs, and other IP rights.
intellectual.description2: Our services include legal advice on IP registration, rights management, licensing, and enforcement of IP rights in cases of infringement, including legal representation in disputes.

labor.title: Employment Law
labor.description1: Verdiyan Legal has experience representing both employers and employees in employment-related disputes, both in pre-litigation stages and in court proceedings.
labor.description2: We provide legal advice on matters such as termination of employment, employment contracts, compensation for damages, internal workplace policies, and employment disputes.

advertising.title: Advertising and Tobacco Law
advertising.description1: We provide specialized legal advice in the area of advertising regulation and tobacco regulation, including related administrative proceedings and communication with regulatory authorities.
advertising.description2: Our law firm assists clients in assessing the legal compliance of marketing campaigns, resolving disputes, and establishing internal compliance processes. Our expertise in this area is supported by long-term cooperation with multinational companies.

bannerSection.title: Consumer Representation in Disputes with Non-Bank Lenders
bannerSection.description: A dedicated area of our legal practice focuses on representing consumers in disputes with consumer credit providers, particularly non-bank lenders. These legal services are provided under the commercial brand Nourox (formerly Finhelp).

aboutUs.title: Verdiyan Legal
aboutUs.paragraph1: The founding partner of Verdiyan Legal is Mgr. Bagrat Verdiyan, an attorney who has been practicing law since 2005 and has worked as an independent attorney since 2009. Before establishing his own law practice, he gained extensive experience through long-term cooperation with a leading Czech law firm, where he was involved in handling legal matters for major corporate clients and representing them in complex litigation.
aboutUs.paragraph2: Verdiyan Legal builds on this experience and provides legal services both as part of ongoing legal advisory and in handling individual cases. We offer professional legal support in business transactions, litigation, protection of property rights, as well as in sensitive personal and family matters. Our priority is to deliver legal services that are not only legally precise but also practical and effective.
aboutUs.paragraph3: Our law firm provides legal services in Czech, English, Russian, and German.

item1.title: Debt recovery and legal representation for both creditors and debtors

item2.title: Legal due diligence and support in business and corporate transactions

item3.title: Representation of clients in court litigation and arbitration proceedings

item4.title: Protection of intellectual property rights and IP dispute resolution

item5.title: Legal representation of Czech and multinational companies

cookies.title: This website uses cookies
cookies.description: We use cookies, which we process according to the principles of personal data protection.

office.title: Office
office.registration: Registered in the Commercial Register maintained by the Municipal Court in Prague, Section C, File No. 12345.

consumerInfo.title: Information for Consumers
consumerInfo.description: The Czech Bar Association has been authorized by the Ministry of Industry and Trade of the Czech Republic to provide out-of-court resolution of consumer disputes in accordance with Act No. 634/1992 Coll., on Consumer Protection, as amended. This authorization applies to disputes between a legal service provider and a consumer concerning the provision of legal services. More information is available on the website of the Czech Bar Association:

introduction.title: Purpose of the Document and Personal Data Protection
introduction.paragraph1: The purpose of this document is to provide clients and other persons with information on personal data processing within the scope set out in Article 13 of Regulation (EU) 2016/679 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the protection of natural persons with regard to the processing of personal data and on the free movement of such data, and repealing Directive 95/46/EC (General Data Protection Regulation) (hereinafter referred to as "GDPR" or "Regulation"), in particular information on what personal data we process, on what legal basis we process it, for what purposes we use it, to whom we transfer it, and what your rights are in relation to the personal data we process.
introduction.paragraph2: The law firm, as the controller of personal data that will be provided to it, undertakes to process such personal data in accordance with legal regulations, in particular Act No. 85/1996 Coll. (Act on Advocacy), Act No. 110/2019 Coll., on Personal Data Processing, and the Regulation. All persons who participate in the processing of personal data within the scope of their work or contractual obligations are subject to legal or contractual confidentiality obligations.

controller.title: IDENTITY AND CONTACT DETAILS OF THE PERSONAL DATA CONTROLLER
controller.intro: The controller of your personal data is
controller.name: Name:

purpose.title: PURPOSE OF PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
purpose.intro: The purpose of processing your personal data is
purpose.item1: fulfillment of contractual obligations under a contract concluded between you and the controller, in particular in connection with the legal service provided. This purpose also includes keeping records in the controller's client database and subsequent archiving of data in accordance with legal regulations.
purpose.item2: fulfillment of obligations established by legal regulations in connection with the legal service provided.

legalBasis.title: LEGAL BASIS FOR PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
legalBasis.intro: The legal basis for processing personal data is:
legalBasis.item1: Contract for the provision of legal services - processing is necessary for the performance of a contract between you and the controller (or for taking steps by the controller prior to entering into such a contract) within the meaning of Article 6(1)(b) GDPR.
legalBasis.item2: processing of personal data necessary for compliance with obligations established by legal regulations within the meaning of Article 6(1)(c) GDPR.

recipients.title: CATEGORIES OF PERSONAL DATA RECIPIENTS
recipients.intro: The controller may further transfer personal data to third parties if this follows from legal regulations or from the nature of the given personal data processing, which may include:
recipients.item1: public authorities (e.g., courts, criminal prosecution authorities, enforcement officers, administrative authorities, supervisory authorities)
recipients.item2: persons participating in the delivery of services or payment processing under a contract concluded with the data subject,
recipients.item3: entities ensuring maintenance of the controller's information system,
recipients.item4: other recipients according to the needs and instructions of the client.

retention.title: PERIOD OF PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
retention.text: Your personal data will be processed for the duration of the contractual relationship and, after its termination, will be handled in accordance with applicable legal regulations, in particular Act No. 85/1996 Coll. (Act on Advocacy), Act No. 499/2004 Coll. (Act on Archiving and Records Management and on Amendments to Certain Acts) and the Regulation.

rights.title: RIGHTS in connection with Personal Data Processing
rights.intro: Under the conditions set out in Articles 15 to 22 GDPR, you have the right
rights.item1: to request access to your personal data processed by the controller,
rights.item2: to request a copy of such personal data,
rights.item3: to request correction of inaccurate personal data,
rights.item4: to request erasure of personal data processed by the controller if they are no longer necessary for the purpose for which they were provided, or if they are processed unlawfully by the controller,
rights.item5: to request restriction of processing of your personal data in cases set out in Article 18 GDPR,
rights.item6: to data portability of your personal data that you have provided on the basis of consent or a contract, and in this connection to have them provided in a commonly used format,
rights.item7: to object to processing of your personal data for direct marketing purposes, including profiling, if profiling relates to such direct marketing. If you object to processing of your personal data for direct marketing purposes, your personal data will no longer be processed for these purposes.
rights.paragraph1: The right to withdraw consent to personal data processing at any time does not apply, as the client's personal data are processed for the purpose of fulfilling a contract concluded with the client, not on the basis of consent to processing.
rights.paragraph2: If you believe that processing of your personal data has violated or is violating the GDPR Regulation, you have, among other things, the right to file a complaint with the supervisory authority, which is the Office for Personal Data Protection.
rights.paragraph3: You can exercise your rights in person at the controller's registered office, or at the address indicated in writing with a certified signature or by email to the controller's email address indicated above, or through the controller's data mailbox. The controller is authorized and obliged to request verification of your identity in connection with the exercise of your rights.

consent.title: Consent
consent.subtitle: This website uses cookies
consent.text: On the law firm's website, we use cookies to analyze website traffic and obtain statistical data. We need your active consent to use them. You can, of course, visit the pages with only necessary cookies; however, in such a case, we cannot guarantee that all integrated services, especially external ones, will work reliably.

cookiesSettings.title: Cookie Settings
cookiesSettings.subtitle: More about cookies
cookiesSettings.text1: Cookies are small text files that can be used by websites to make the user experience more efficient.
cookiesSettings.text2: The law states that we can store cookies on your device if they are strictly necessary for the operation of this site. For all other types of cookies, we need your permission.
cookiesSettings.text3: This site uses various types of cookies. Some cookies are placed by third-party services that appear on our pages.
cookiesSettings.text4: You can change or withdraw your consent at any time through the Cookie Statement on our website.
cookiesSettings.text5: Find out more in our privacy policy about who we are, how you can Contact Us, and how we process your personal data.
cookiesSettings.text6: Please provide your consent ID and the date when you will Contact Us regarding your consent.
cookiesSettings.necessaryDesc: Necessary cookies help make a website usable by enabling basic functions such as page navigation and access to secure areas of the website. The website cannot function properly without these cookies.
cookiesSettings.preferentialDesc: Preferential cookies allow a website to remember information that changes how the website behaves or looks. This is, for example, your preferred language or the region where you are located.
cookiesSettings.statisticalDesc: Statistical cookies help website owners understand how visitors use websites. They collect and report information anonymously.
cookiesSettings.marketingDesc: Marketing cookies are used to track visitors on websites. The intention is to display ads that are relevant and interesting to individual users and thus more valuable for publishers and third-party advertisers.
cookiesSettings.unclassifiedDesc: Unclassified cookies are cookies that we are in the process of classifying together with the providers of individual cookies.
